compiled_state.author: Joe Oakhart
compiled_state.description:
## Reproduction Steps

1. In a disposable repository, add an activated `code_review` entry for
   `project.review-phase-boundaries` to `.rebar/criteria_routing.json` with
   `exec: "AGENT"`, `block_threshold: 0.90`, and `blocking_enabled: false`.
2. Add the matching project prompt and eval spec under `.rebar/prompts/` and
   `.rebar/evals/`.
3. Run the four-pass code-review workflow over a matching prompt/reviewer diff, then run the
   per-criterion eval command for the activated project criterion.

## Expected vs Actual

**Expected:** the activated project criterion is resolved from the reviewed repository, runs
exactly once in Pass 1, carries its logical `project.*` criterion id into findings, is judged by
Pass 3 using the repository's effective routing, and can be evaluated through the existing
per-criterion eval surface.

**Actual:** `gates/code-review.yaml` contains only a static built-in prompt list;
`CodeReviewBatchRunner` iterates only that list; `code_review_decide` calls
`registry.threshold_for` and `nit_suppressed_criteria` without the repository's effective routing;
and `eval_solver._code_review_prompt_id` accepts only the base, verifier, and packaged overlay ids.
The overlay vocabulary therefore advertises net-new code-review project criteria that cannot run or
control their configured posture end to end.

## Root Cause

ADR 0017 completed the shared overlay merge and exposed `code_review.effective_criteria` /
`effective_routing`, but code review never received the runner-side dynamic fan-in that plan review
uses, nor repo-aware Pass-3 lookup or project-id eval resolution. This is a generic integration gap,
not a special case for the boundary criterion.

## Scope

- Add `CODE_REVIEW_PROMPT_PREFIX = "code-review-"` and a gate-keyed
  `criterion_prompt_id(criterion_id, *, gate_key="plan_review")`; the default preserves existing
  plan-review callers while code-review `project.<name>` maps to
  `code-review-project-<name-with-dots-replaced-by-dashes>`.
- Fan activated non-DET code-review project criteria into `round_a` only.
  `CodeReviewBatchRunner` uses `PROJECT_FAN_IN_STEP_ID = "round_a"` as a stateless discriminator,
  deduplicates physical ids against static criteria, and never injects them for `round_b`; no mutable
  flag survives between reviews. Do not change the version-pinned workflow schema or hard-code
  project ids in packaged YAML.
- Resolve project prompts from the reviewed snapshot; a missing or malformed activated prompt
  raises a located `LLMError` before model execution rather than being skipped or converted to an
  empty-findings coverage gap.
- Bind Pass 3 threshold and nit-suppression lookup to `effective_routing(ctx.repo_root)`.
- Extend the existing criterion-eval resolver to recognize activated code-review project criteria
  and load their `.rebar/evals/<prompt-id>.eval.yaml` specs. The resolver reuses
  `_run_code_review_case`, reads each case's `diff` key, and returns the prompt's native findings;
  it does not route project code-review criteria through plan-review `pass1_chunk` or add a new arm.
- Extend the `criteria eval` CLI preflight to accept a logical code-review project id only when it
  appears in `code_review.effective_criteria(repo_root)`. Compare membership in plan-review and
  code-review registries: no match is unknown, exactly one selects that gate, and a dual-gate match
  is an explicit ambiguity error. Pass the selected gate through `calibrate_criterion` to the
  gate-keyed prompt mapper; unknown, inactive, and ambiguous ids remain errors.
- Extend calibration with
  `calibrate_criterion(criterion_id, *, repo_root=None, runs=1, gate_key="plan_review")`; the CLI
  passes its uniquely resolved gate and calibration calls
  `criterion_prompt_id(criterion_id, gate_key=gate_key)`.
- When a project prompt emits findings, overwrite each finding's `criteria` with its logical
  `project.*` id and set `reviewer_id` to the physical prompt id. Do not trust or translate the
  model's criterion attribution. Built-in findings preserve their current behavior.
- Update `docs/llm-framework.md` with the stable naming/runtime/error/eval contract.
- Preserve byte-for-byte behavior for repositories without a code-review project overlay.

Out of scope: authoring `project.review-phase-boundaries`, changing the four-pass decision math,
changing packaged built-in criteria, or deploying a Gerrit dogfood criterion.

## Implementation Notes

Likely seams are `src/rebar/llm/criteria/ids.py`,
`src/rebar/llm/code_review/batch_runner.py`,
`src/rebar/llm/code_review/workflow_ops.py`, and
`src/rebar/llm/evals/eval_solver.py`, `src/rebar/llm/evals/eval.py`, plus the CLI preflight in
`src/rebar/_cli/_llm_eval_commands.py`. Reuse the existing plan-review runner-side fan-in pattern;
do not add a project-specific branch or a new workflow DSL field. Eval recognition must invert the
same sanitized mapping and confirm the logical id is active in the repository-effective registry;
a prefix match alone is insufficient. `_code_review_prompt_id` therefore receives `repo_root`,
builds an active logical-to-physical map with `criterion_prompt_id(..., gate_key="code_review")`,
and returns the input physical id only when it is one of those computed values. This avoids parsing
the forward-only filename back into a logical id.

In `code_review_decide`, compute `routing = registry.effective_routing(ctx.repo_root)` once, pass a
callback using that map to `pass3_over_findings` (`threshold_for` returns threshold plus blocking
posture), and pass the same map to
`nit_suppressed_criteria`. Both threshold/posture and nit suppression must be repo-aware together.
The authoring preview/editor remain plan-review-only and use the mapper's default; adding a visual
code-review criterion editor is out of scope.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] An offline full-gate test activates a synthetic code-review `project.*` criterion and proves
  its project prompt runs once per review across reusable-runner `round_a` and `round_b` requests,
  every emitted finding carries the logical id in `criteria`, and the physical prompt/eval ids pass
  validation; verify with
  `.venv/bin/pytest -q tests/unit/test_code_review_project_criteria.py -k fan_in`.
- [ ] A finding tagged only with the synthetic project id records the overlay's configured
  `block_threshold` and `blocking_enabled` values in Pass 3, and overlay `nit_suppressed` overrides
  are honored; verify with
  `.venv/bin/pytest -q tests/unit/test_code_review_project_criteria.py -k routing`.
- [ ] An activated project criterion with a missing or malformed prompt fails with a located error,
  while an overlay-absent repository produces the pre-change workflow result; verify with
  `.venv/bin/pytest -q tests/unit/test_code_review_project_criteria.py -k 'missing or parity'`.
- [ ] The criterion eval resolver accepts an activated code-review project id and its sanitized
  prompt id, the CLI preflight admits the logical id, the repository eval spec loads, and unknown or
  inactive ids plus a dual-gate ambiguous id are rejected. Its `diff` fixture runs through
  `_run_code_review_case` and produces native findings without entering the plan-review criterion
  arm; verify with
  `.venv/bin/pytest -q tests/unit/test_code_review_project_criteria.py -k 'eval or cli'`.
- [ ] Existing shared-overlay and four-pass code-review regression suites remain green; verify with
  `.venv/bin/pytest -q tests/unit/test_unified_criteria.py tests/unit/test_code_review_evals.py
  tests/unit/workflow/test_code_review_workflow.py`.
- [ ] `docs/llm-framework.md` contains a `Code-review project criteria` section covering Round-A
  fan-in, logical/physical ids, fatal configuration errors, deterministic Pass-3 routing, and the
  `criteria eval` entry point; verify with
  `.venv/bin/python -c 'from pathlib import Path; s=Path("docs/llm-framework.md").read_text(); assert
  all(x in s for x in ("Code-review project criteria", "round_a", "criteria eval",
  "configuration error", "effective routing"))' && make lint`.

## Rollback

Revert the additive fan-in, repo-aware lookup, and eval-resolution changes together. Repositories
without a project code-review overlay remain the compatibility baseline, so rollback requires no
data migration or cleanup.

file_impact.reason: Collision-safe gate-keyed project criterion id mapping
Stateless Round-A fan-in and logical finding attribution
Use one repository-effective Pass-3 routing map
Dispatch activated project eval specifications through the code-review arm
Select the physical prompt id from the resolved criterion gate
Admit one-gate logical ids and reject unknown or ambiguous ids
Reproduce and pin the repaired integration seam

compiled_state.title: Project code-review criteria do not run through the full four-pass integration seam

verify_commands.dd_text: Project criterion executes once per review and stamps logical criterion ids

verify_commands.command: rtk .venv/bin/pytest -q tests/unit/test_code_review_project_criteria.py -k "missing or parity or eval or cli"
verify_commands.dd_text: Fatal errors, parity, eval resolution, and CLI gate selection pass

verify_commands.command: rtk .venv/bin/python -c "from pathlib import Path; s=Path(\"docs/llm-framework.md\").read_text(); assert all(x in s for x in (\"Code-review project criteria\", \"round_a\", \"criteria eval\", \"configuration error\", \"effective routing\"))"
verify_commands.dd_text: Runtime documentation covers the stable project-criterion contract
